Detect faults in the assets your sensors can't reach

Offshore platforms, ATEX zones, ESPs kilometres underground, nacelles 100 metres up. SAM4 monitors them all from the motor control cabinet. No sensors on the asset. No permits required.

Deployments across oil & gas, wind, and thermal energy. 90.8% detection rate measured across the full SAM4 fleet. See results ↓

See How It Works Talk to an engineer

Trusted by energy leaders

The challenge

Energy assets operate where sensors can't go

ATEX zones, nacelles 100 metres up, ESPs kilometres underground. Vibration sensors can't reach these assets. SAM4 monitors from the motor control cabinet.

ATEX zone access constraints

Placing sensors on assets in explosive atmospheres is dangerous and often prohibited. SAM4 monitors from the motor control cabinet, safely outside hazardous zones.

Electric submersible pump failures

ESPs operate deep underground with no physical access. Failures mean lost production and expensive workovers. SAM4 monitors the full ESP drivetrain from surface.

Read our SPE paper →

Unplanned downtime costs

A single compressor failure can halt an entire processing train. SAM4 provides weeks-to-months advance warning on developing faults across the full drivetrain.

Wind turbine generator faults

Nacelle-mounted generators and gearboxes are expensive to access. SAM4 reads the electrical signature at the base controller, detecting bearing wear, winding faults, and gearbox degradation without climbing the tower.

Co-development opportunity →

Thermal plant reliability

Boiler feed pumps, cooling water systems, and forced-draft fans run continuously in thermal plants. Failures cascade into lost generation. SAM4 monitors the full motor-driven drivetrain from the MCC.

Co-development opportunity →

Seawater lift pump failures

Seawater lift pumps operate in corrosive offshore environments with limited physical access. Failures disrupt cooling and injection systems across the platform. SAM4 detects pump degradation, seal wear, and motor faults from the MCC. No subsea intervention required.

Read our SPE paper →

What we monitor

Critical assets across energy production

ESPs

Electric submersible pumps monitored from surface, detecting motor degradation, cable faults, and pump wear without downhole access.

Learn more →

Compressors

Gas and process compressors with bearing, coupling, and gearbox fault detection, critical for continuous production.

Learn more →

Pumps

Injection, transfer, boiler feed, and cooling water pumps with cavitation detection, seal wear alerts, and efficiency monitoring.

Learn more →

Fans & blowers

Forced-draft and induced-draft fans in thermal plants, plus cooling tower fans. Bearing, imbalance, and belt-drive monitoring.

Learn more →

MV motors

Medium-voltage motors driving large compressors, generators, and process-critical equipment across energy facilities.

Learn more →

Don’t see your asset?

We co-develop SAM4 for new use cases. Bring yours. 5 partner slots open for 2026.

Explore the programme →

Deployments

Results from energy production deployments

90.8% Detection rate (Jan 2024 – Feb 2026)
5 months Maximum advance warning before failure
5-35% Energy waste identified
60–90 min Installation time per asset

These metrics are from initial deployments. The 90.8% detection rate and 0.32% false communication rate are measured across the full SAM4 fleet. As fleet data grows in energy production, we'll publish segment-specific accuracy.

Proof

Energy production case studies

1 Critical ESP failure prevented

Detecting a broken sine wave filter in ESPs

SAM4 identified an electrical fault in an ESP system that would have caused complete failure and costly workover.

Read case study →
3 Fault types detected

Three faults detected on oil transfer pumps

SAM4 detected bearing degradation, misalignment, and electrical imbalance on oil transfer pumps before any of them caused failure.

Read case study →

How it works

Simple installation, immediate insights

SAM4 installs inside the motor control cabinet. No sensors in ATEX zones, no nacelle access, no production shutdown.

  1. Install in the MCC Clip-on current and voltage sensors attach to existing wiring in the motor control cabinet. Takes 60–90 minutes per asset.
  2. Connect to SAM4 cloud The DAQ device samples at 20 kHz and sends data securely to the SAM4 platform for AI analysis.
  3. Get actionable alerts Receive predictive alerts weeks to months before failure, with fault type, severity, and recommended action.
See how it installs →

From a single offshore platform to your entire asset base

Start with ESPs and hazardous-zone pumps. Once SAM4 is in the MCC, extending coverage to compressors, fans, and auxiliary systems costs near-zero marginal effort.

See How It Scales

Start with a paid validation

SAM4-X lets you prove the technology on your assets before committing to a fleet-wide contract. Fixed scope. Fixed price. Real results on your equipment, not a generic demo.
